prompt:
あなたはTOEIC Part 6のテスト作成専門家です。
長文穴埋め問題を作成してください。

TOEIC Part 6の形式：
- 1つの文書（メール、記事、広告など）に4つの空欄
- 各空欄に4つの選択肢（A, B, C, D）
- 語彙・文法・文脈理解を総合的に評価
- ビジネス文書形式

重要な要求事項：
1. 自然で実用的なビジネス文書を作成
2. 文法的に正確で、ネイティブスピーカーが自然に感じる表現
3. TOEIC Part 6の形式と難易度に準拠
4. 4つの空欄を適切な位置に配置
5. 各空欄に4つの選択肢を提供
6. 正解は1つのみ、他は明確に不正解
7. 日本語解説も含める

出力形式はJSON形式で以下の構造にしてください：
{
  "documentType": "指定された文書タイプ",
  "title": "文書のタイトル",
  "fullText": "空欄を(1)、(2)、(3)、(4)で示した完全な文書",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": ["選択肢A", "選択肢B", "選択肢C", "選択肢D"],
      "correct": "A|B|C|D",
      "explanation": "なぜその答えが正しいかの説明（日本語）",
      "questionType": "vocabulary|grammar|context|sentence_insertion"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "easy|medium|hard",
  "topic": "ビジネス分野",
  "wordCount": 文書の単語数
}

以下の条件でTOEIC Part 6問題を1問作成してください：

【生成条件】
- 難易度: hard
- 文書タイプ: article (Article/Report)
- ビジネストピック: service_announcements (サービス変更通知)
- 空欄数: 4個

【特別な要求】
各空欄の正解位置は以下のように配置してください：
- 空欄1の正解位置: A
- 空欄2の正解位置: A
- 空欄3の正解位置: D
- 空欄4の正解位置: B

【問題タイプの配分】
- 空欄1: grammar (文法・語形)
- 空欄2: vocabulary (語彙選択)
- 空欄3: context (文脈理解)
- 空欄4: sentence_insertion (文挿入)

JSON形式で問題を作成してください。

{
  "documentType": "article (Article/Report)",
  "title": "Upcoming Revisions to Our Premium Support Service",
  "fullText": "Subject: Upcoming Revisions to Our Premium Support Service\n\nWe are writing to inform clients of upcoming revisions to the Premium Support service that will take effect on November 1. These changes are intended to streamline issue resolution, reduce response times, and deliver enhanced technical assistance.\n\n(1) All existing subscribers ______ to the new platform; however, they may need to reauthenticate their accounts through a one-time verification process. The migration will occur in phases to minimize disruption, and our team will provide detailed instructions and a timeline.\n\n(2) Effective date adjustments include a revised tier structure and an updated pricing model. The new tiers introduce ______ response windows, with enterprise customers receiving guaranteed four-hour initial responses, while standard customers will continue to receive next-business-day assistance. We believe this reconfiguration will better align resources with customer needs.\n\nFor clients who currently rely on legacy integrations, we will offer a transition toolkit consisting of API adapters, documentation, and dedicated migration support. Please note that legacy endpoints will be deprecated on the effective date, and (3) ______.\n\nAdditionally, we are expanding our regional support coverage to include Asia-Pacific hours, enabling follow-the-sun assistance for critical incidents. (4) ______ Our customer success managers will be reaching out to coordinate any necessary adjustments.\n\nIf you have questions regarding these updates or require personalized support planning, please contact your account manager or email support-updates@example.com. We appreciate your cooperation and look forward to continuing to serve your organization.",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": [
        "will automatically be migrated",
        "will automatically migrate",
        "are automatically migrating",
        "have been automatically migrated"
      ],
      "correct": "A",
      "explanation": "正解は A。「subscribers（利用者）」がプラットフォーム側へ移行されることを受け身で表すため、\"will ... be migrated\" の受動態が最も自然で正確です。B（will automatically migrate）は能動態で \"subscribers\" 自ら移行するような意味になり不自然、C（are automatically migrating）は現在進行形で予定のニュアンスと合わない、D（have been automatically migrated）は既に完了していることを示し、施行日が将来である本文と矛盾します。",
      "questionType": "grammar"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank2",
      "position": 2,
      "options": [
        "differentiated",
        "prominent",
        "consecutive",
        "unified"
      ],
      "correct": "A",
      "explanation": "正解は A。「differentiate（区別する）」の形容詞形 \"differentiated response windows\" は、各顧客層に応じて応答時間を区別することを意味し文脈に合致します。B（prominent）は「目立つ」、C（consecutive）は「連続した」、D（unified）は「統一された」で、いずれも \"response windows\" の意味や次の文（企業顧客は4時間など）と整合しません。",
      "questionType": "vocabulary"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank3",
      "position": 3,
      "options": [
        "clients will be responsible for maintaining legacy endpoints indefinitely.",
        "we will continue to support both legacy and updated endpoints permanently.",
        "no further assistance will be available for any integration issues.",
        "we therefore urge you to begin migration as soon as possible to avoid service disruptions."
      ],
      "correct": "D",
      "explanation": "正解は D。前の文で「legacy endpoints will be deprecated（廃止予定）」と述べているため、その結果として顧客に対して速やかな移行を促す文（we therefore urge you ...）が自然に続きます。A は永続的に維持することを示し矛盾、B は廃止と矛盾、C は極端かつ不親切で文脈にそぐいません。",
      "questionType": "context"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank4",
      "position": 4,
      "options": [
        "This change will not affect customers outside the continental United States.",
        "We encourage clients to review the updated service level agreement and confirm their preferred escalation contacts.",
        "All billing invoices will be processed through a third-party vendor as of January.",
        "Live chat support will be discontinued."
      ],
      "correct": "B",
      "explanation": "正解は B。この段落はサポート拡大と調整に関する案内なので、SLA を確認しエスカレーション連絡先を確定するよう促す文が自然につながります。A は地域に関する断定で文脈に直接関連しない可能性が高く、C は請求処理に関する別トピック、D は“サポート拡大”という流れと正反対の内容で不適切です。",
      "questionType": "sentence_insertion"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "hard",
  "topic": "service_announcements (サービス変更通知)",
  "wordCount": 239
}

あなたはTOEIC Part 6問題の翻訳専門家です。
文書全体と選択肢を適切に日本語に翻訳してください。

重要なルール：
1. 文書は自然で分かりやすい日本語に翻訳
2. 与えられた文書は既に完成した文章なので、そのまま翻訳
3. 選択肢は1-4語の簡潔な日本語に翻訳
4. 選択肢に説明や解説は絶対に不要
5. 文脈に応じた最も適切な意味のみ
6. ビジネス用語は適切な日本語表現を使用
7. 4つの空欄すべてに対する選択肢翻訳を提供すること

出力形式：
{
  "titleTranslation": "タイトルの翻訳",
  "contentTranslation": "文書全体の翻訳（空欄なしの完全な文章として）",
  "questionsTranslations": [
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    },
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    },
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    },
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    }
  ]
}

次のTOEIC Part 6問題を翻訳してください：

タイトル：Upcoming Revisions to Our Premium Support Service

文書全体（正解が埋められた完全な文章）：
Subject: Upcoming Revisions to Our Premium Support Service

We are writing to inform clients of upcoming revisions to the Premium Support service that will take effect on November 1. These changes are intended to streamline issue resolution, reduce response times, and deliver enhanced technical assistance.

will automatically be migrated All existing subscribers ______ to the new platform; however, they may need to reauthenticate their accounts through a one-time verification process. The migration will occur in phases to minimize disruption, and our team will provide detailed instructions and a timeline.

differentiated Effective date adjustments include a revised tier structure and an updated pricing model. The new tiers introduce ______ response windows, with enterprise customers receiving guaranteed four-hour initial responses, while standard customers will continue to receive next-business-day assistance. We believe this reconfiguration will better align resources with customer needs.

For clients who currently rely on legacy integrations, we will offer a transition toolkit consisting of API adapters, documentation, and dedicated migration support. Please note that legacy endpoints will be deprecated on the effective date, and we therefore urge you to begin migration as soon as possible to avoid service disruptions. ______.

Additionally, we are expanding our regional support coverage to include Asia-Pacific hours, enabling follow-the-sun assistance for critical incidents. We encourage clients to review the updated service level agreement and confirm their preferred escalation contacts. ______ Our customer success managers will be reaching out to coordinate any necessary adjustments.

If you have questions regarding these updates or require personalized support planning, please contact your account manager or email support-updates@example.com. We appreciate your cooperation and look forward to continuing to serve your organization.

選択肢：
空欄1: A. will automatically be migrated, B. will automatically migrate, C. are automatically migrating, D. have been automatically migrated
空欄2: A. differentiated, B. prominent, C. consecutive, D. unified
空欄3: A. clients will be responsible for maintaining legacy endpoints indefinitely., B. we will continue to support both legacy and updated endpoints permanently., C. no further assistance will be available for any integration issues., D. we therefore urge you to begin migration as soon as possible to avoid service disruptions.
空欄4: A. This change will not affect customers outside the continental United States., B. We encourage clients to review the updated service level agreement and confirm their preferred escalation contacts., C. All billing invoices will be processed through a third-party vendor as of January., D. Live chat support will be discontinued.

重要：
1. 上記の文書は既に空欄に正解が埋められた完全な文章です
2. この完全な文章をそのまま自然な日本語に翻訳してください
3. 翻訳には空欄を含めないでください（元の文書は既に完成しています）
4. 選択肢は簡潔に翻訳してください
5. questionsTranslationsは必ず4つの要素を含む配列で、各要素が4つの選択肢翻訳を含むこと

JSON形式で翻訳を出力してください。

あなたはTOEIC Part 6のテスト作成専門家です。
長文穴埋め問題を作成してください。

TOEIC Part 6の形式：
- 1つの文書（メール、記事、広告など）に4つの空欄
- 各空欄に4つの選択肢（A, B, C, D）
- 語彙・文法・文脈理解を総合的に評価
- ビジネス文書形式

重要な要求事項：
1. 自然で実用的なビジネス文書を作成
2. 文法的に正確で、ネイティブスピーカーが自然に感じる表現
3. TOEIC Part 6の形式と難易度に準拠
4. 4つの空欄を適切な位置に配置
5. 各空欄に4つの選択肢を提供
6. 正解は1つのみ、他は明確に不正解
7. 日本語解説も含める

出力形式はJSON形式で以下の構造にしてください：
{
  "documentType": "指定された文書タイプ",
  "title": "文書のタイトル",
  "fullText": "空欄を(1)、(2)、(3)、(4)で示した完全な文書",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": ["選択肢A", "選択肢B", "選択肢C", "選択肢D"],
      "correct": "A|B|C|D",
      "explanation": "なぜその答えが正しいかの説明（日本語）",
      "questionType": "vocabulary|grammar|context|sentence_insertion"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "easy|medium|hard",
  "topic": "ビジネス分野",
  "wordCount": 文書の単語数
}

以下の条件でTOEIC Part 6問題を1問作成してください：

【生成条件】
- 難易度: hard
- 文書タイプ: advertisement (Advertisement)
- ビジネストピック: training_programs (研修プログラム)
- 空欄数: 4個

【特別な要求】
各空欄の正解位置は以下のように配置してください：
- 空欄1の正解位置: C
- 空欄2の正解位置: D
- 空欄3の正解位置: D
- 空欄4の正解位置: B

【問題タイプの配分】
- 空欄1: vocabulary (語彙選択)
- 空欄2: grammar (文法・語形)
- 空欄3: sentence_insertion (文挿入)
- 空欄4: context (文脈理解)

JSON形式で問題を作成してください。

{
  "documentType": "Advertisement",
  "title": "Executive Development: Advanced Leadership Programs",
  "fullText": "Executive Development: Advanced Leadership Programs\n\nAre you ready to elevate your organization's leadership capacity? Our Advanced Leadership Programs offer intensive, cohort-based training for mid- to senior-level managers. The curriculum is research-driven and tailored to address contemporary challenges such as remote team management, strategic decision-making, and cross-cultural communication. Each module combines case studies, interactive workshops, and mentorship to deliver skills that are immediately (1) in the workplace.\n\nPrograms run over eight weeks with a blend of synchronous sessions and asynchronous assignments. Sessions are led by industry practitioners and academic experts, and attendance is monitored to guarantee cohort cohesion. Participants who successfully complete all required components (2) eligible to receive a professional certification recognized by major industry associations.\n\n(3)\n\nEnrollment is limited to cohorts of 20 to maintain instructional quality. Early-bird tuition includes access to supplemental digital resources and two one-on-one coaching sessions; corporate packages that include on-site delivery and post-program analytics are also available. For pricing and group rates, please contact our program coordinator at programs@example.com or visit our website to download the detailed syllabus. We recommend securing your spot early, as spaces fill quickly. (4)",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": [
        "theoretical",
        "obscure",
        "actionable",
        "ambiguous"
      ],
      "correct": "C",
      "explanation": "「immediately (1) in the workplace」の文脈では、習得した技能がすぐに職場で利用できることを表す語が必要です。actionable（実行可能な、すぐに活用できる）が最も自然です。他の選択肢は意味が合いません（theoretical＝理論的、obscure＝不明瞭、ambiguous＝曖昧）。",
      "questionType": "vocabulary"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank2",
      "position": 2,
      "options": [
        "are being",
        "have been",
        "would be",
        "will be"
      ],
      "correct": "D",
      "explanation": "文全体は「Participants who successfully complete all required components ___ eligible to receive ...」で、将来の結果（修了後に資格を受け取る資格を得る）を述べるため、正しい語形は受動の未来形 \"will be eligible\" です。他は時制や態の使い方が不適切です。",
      "questionType": "grammar"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank3",
      "position": 3,
      "options": [
        "We do not provide any post-course materials or follow-up services.",
        "Meals and accommodations are included with every in-person session.",
        "Applicants must complete an online assessment before enrollment is confirmed.",
        "A capstone project, assessed by a panel of instructors and industry advisors during the final week, is required to determine certification eligibility."
      ],
      "correct": "D",
      "explanation": "前後の文脈は「修了者が専門資格を受け取る」といった認定に関する記述であり、直後に資格判定の方法を説明する文が続くのが自然です。選択肢Dはその役割を果たし、資格取得の条件（カプストーン評価）を明確にしています。他の選択肢は文脈に直接つながらないか矛盾します。",
      "questionType": "sentence_insertion"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank4",
      "position": 4,
      "options": [
        "Note that travel expenses are covered for all participants.",
        "Payment plans are available for qualifying organizations.",
        "Only credit cards are accepted for registration.",
        "Walk-in registration on the first day is permitted."
      ],
      "correct": "B",
      "explanation": "直前で「For pricing and group rates, please contact...」とあるため、支払い方法や料金に関する補足が自然です。選択肢B（条件を満たす組織向けの分割支払いプランの提供）は文脈に合致します。他の選択肢は誤解を招く記述（経費全額負担や単一の支払い方法、事前登録不要の意味）で、文脈からの補足として適切ではありません。",
      "questionType": "context"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "hard",
  "topic": "training_programs",
  "wordCount": 185
}

文書全体と選択肢を適切に日本語に翻訳してください。

1. 文書は自然で分かりやすい日本語に翻訳
2. 与えられた文書は既に完成した文章なので、そのまま翻訳

次のTOEIC Part 6問題を翻訳してください：

タイトル：Executive Development: Advanced Leadership Programs

文書全体（正解が埋められた完全な文章）：
Executive Development: Advanced Leadership Programs

Are you ready to elevate your organization's leadership capacity? Our Advanced Leadership Programs offer intensive, cohort-based training for mid- to senior-level managers. The curriculum is research-driven and tailored to address contemporary challenges such as remote team management, strategic decision-making, and cross-cultural communication. Each module combines case studies, interactive workshops, and mentorship to deliver skills that are immediately actionable in the workplace.

Programs run over eight weeks with a blend of synchronous sessions and asynchronous assignments. Sessions are led by industry practitioners and academic experts, and attendance is monitored to guarantee cohort cohesion. Participants who successfully complete all required components will be eligible to receive a professional certification recognized by major industry associations.

A capstone project, assessed by a panel of instructors and industry advisors during the final week, is required to determine certification eligibility.

Enrollment is limited to cohorts of 20 to maintain instructional quality. Early-bird tuition includes access to supplemental digital resources and two one-on-one coaching sessions; corporate packages that include on-site delivery and post-program analytics are also available. For pricing and group rates, please contact our program coordinator at programs@example.com or visit our website to download the detailed syllabus. We recommend securing your spot early, as spaces fill quickly. Payment plans are available for qualifying organizations.

選択肢：
空欄1: A. theoretical, B. obscure, C. actionable, D. ambiguous
空欄2: A. are being, B. have been, C. would be, D. will be
空欄3: A. We do not provide any post-course materials or follow-up services., B. Meals and accommodations are included with every in-person session., C. Applicants must complete an online assessment before enrollment is confirmed., D. A capstone project, assessed by a panel of instructors and industry advisors during the final week, is required to determine certification eligibility.
空欄4: A. Note that travel expenses are covered for all participants., B. Payment plans are available for qualifying organizations., C. Only credit cards are accepted for registration., D. Walk-in registration on the first day is permitted.

重要：
1. 上記の文書は既に空欄に正解が埋められた完全な文章です
2. この完全な文章をそのまま自然な日本語に翻訳してください
3. 翻訳には空欄を含めないでください（元の文書は既に完成しています）
4. 選択肢は簡潔に翻訳してください
5. questionsTranslationsは必ず4つの要素を含む配列で、各要素が4つの選択肢翻訳を含むこと

JSON形式で翻訳を出力してください。
